task.title: Connecting to a wifi network by using nmcli

li.p: A wifi device is installed on the host.
The wifi device is enabled. To verify, use the nmcli radio command.

context.p: When you first use the nmcli utility to connect to a wifi network, it automatically creates a NetworkManager connection profile. You can then modify this profile to add specific settings, such as a static IP address.

step.cmd: If the wifi radio has been disabled in NetworkManager, enable this feature:
Optional: Display the available wifi networks:

info.codeblock: # nmcli device wifi list IN-USE BSSID SSID MODE CHAN RATE SIGNAL BARS SECURITY 00:53:00:2F:3B:08 Office Infra 44 270 Mbit/s 57 ▂▄▆_ WPA2 WPA3 00:53:00:15:03:BF -- Infra 1 130 Mbit/s 48 ▂▄__ WPA2 WPA3
info.p: The service set identifier (SSID) column contains the names of the networks. If the column shows --, the access point of this network does not broadcast an SSID.

info.codeblock: # nmcli device wifi connect Office --ask Password: wifi-password
info.p: If you prefer to set the password in the command instead of entering it interactively, use the password <wifi_password> option in the command instead of --ask:
info.codeblock: # nmcli device wifi connect Office password <wifi_password>
info.p: Note that, if the network requires static IP addresses, NetworkManager fails to activate the connection at this point. You can configure the IP addresses in later steps.

step.cmd: If the network requires static IP addresses:

li.p: Configure the IPv4 address settings, for example:
li.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify Office ipv4.method manual ipv4.addresses 192.0.2.1/24 ipv4.gateway 192.0.2.254 ipv4.dns 192.0.2.200 ipv4.dns-search example.com

li.p: Configure the IPv6 address settings, for example:
li.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify Office ipv6.method manual ipv6.addresses 2001:db8:1::1/64 ipv6.gateway 2001:db8:1::fffe ipv6.dns 2001:db8:1::ffbb ipv6.dns-search example.com
# nmcli connection show --active NAME ID TYPE DEVICE Office 2501eb7e-7b16-4dc6-97ef-7cc460139a58 wifi wlp0s20f3
li.p: If the output lists the wifi connection you have created, the connection is active.
Ping a hostname or IP address:
